response.justification: Wei Chen is a full-stack web and platform engineer whose career has been built almost entirely on TypeScript, Node.js, JavaScript, and DLang. The role mandates 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows, but the resume shows only roughly 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments plus a bachelor thesis project — neither on Windows nor at production scale in financial systems. There is no Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, or Capital Markets domain experience; RIDE Capital involves fintech product work in TypeScript, not capital-markets infrastructure. SQL Server and Oracle are both absent from the skills profile. The candidate does not meet the mandatory requirements on any of the three pillars: C++ depth, Windows platform experience, or financial-domain knowledge.
strengths: Demonstrated CI/CD, DevOps, and infrastructure experience (GitLab, Docker, Artifactory) aligns with optional pipeline knowledge
Java background at Altom and embedded/low-latency work at Dunnhumby shows ability to work in performance-sensitive environments
Strong written communication evidenced by public workshops, conference talks, and open-source project documentation
concerns: C++ experience totals roughly 6 months (NI LabWindows, 2014–2015) against a mandatory 5–10 year requirement, with no Windows-platform C++ production work
Zero Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, Investment Banking, or Capital Markets domain exposure — the role requires at least one
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; SQL appears only as a general skill tag with MySQL context, not the enterprise RDBMS platforms required
